description.value:
Your underworld contacts can shelter people, hide goods, and move information through back channels. When you are in a settlement where a criminal underworld or informal network exists, you can spend 1 hour and attempt a Society check against a DC set by the GM, usually based on the settlement’s level and how dangerous or visible your situation is.

On a success, you secure a safehouse, hidden storage, discreet messenger, or illicit route for up to 24 hours. Creatures using the safehouse or route are harder to find through ordinary inquiries: attempts to Gather Information, Track, or otherwise locate them through the settlement’s public channels use your Society DC if it is higher than the normal DC. On a critical success, the arrangement also leaves no obvious trail back to you or your allies unless the pursuer critically succeeds.

This doesn’t protect against magic, direct observation, betrayal by an established NPC, or an enemy who already knows where to look.
